states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial state before authentication or command sequence.

states_list.name: AUTH_PENDING
states_list.description: User name provided, waiting for password (and possibly account).

states_list.name: AUTHENTICATED
states_list.description: Session successfully authenticated; ready for most operations.

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data channel connection details negotiated (Passive or Active).

states_list.name: DATA_TRANSFER
states_list.description: File or directory listing transfer in progress.

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Rename source file specified, waiting for destination name.

invariants_list.rule: PASS must be sent after USER.
Successful USER and PASS authentication must occur before LIST, RETR, or STOR commands.
PASV or PORT should be issued before initiating data transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR.
QUIT is valid at any point in the session to terminate the connection.
If authentication fails (e.g., response 530), the client may restart the login sequence with USER.
RNTO must be preceded by an RNFR command in the same session.
The client must wait for the server's 220 greeting before sending the first command.
PORT.host-port-data: Format: PORT h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (comma separated integers)
TYPE.type-code: One character code: A (ASCII) or I (Image/Binary)
RNFR.pathname: Target file name for rename source
RNTO.pathname: Target file name for rename destination

status_changes.description: PASS must be sent after USER.
status_changes.rationale: Directly stated in the protocol specification as analyzed by the spec agent.

status_changes.description: PASV or PORT should be issued before initiating data transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR.
status_changes.rationale: Standard procedure for establishing the data connection in FTP, supported by spec analysis.

status_changes.description: QUIT is valid at any point in the session to terminate the connection.
status_changes.rationale: Universal termination command in FTP, confirmed by spec analysis.

status_changes.description: If authentication fails (e.g., response 530), the client may restart the login sequence with USER.
status_changes.rationale: Confirmed by session trace analysis where a failed login is followed by a new USER command.
